description: Movies, music, TV, and things that were definitely on the internet.

questions.hostIntro: Cinema! Where we pay $20 to eat $15 popcorn in the dark and call it culture.
questions.question: Which film won the very first Academy Award for Best Picture?
questions.hostCommentary: WINGS (1927)! A WWI aerial war film. It won the first ever Oscar. You are now statistically more cultured than you were 30 seconds ago.

questions.hostIntro: This one is embarrassingly easy. We're putting it in anyway.
questions.question: What band famously asked 'Is this the real life? Is this just fantasy?'
questions.hostCommentary: QUEEN! Bohemian Rhapsody (1975). If you got this wrong, we are genuinely worried about you.

questions.hostIntro: Someone at this table has seen every episode of this show. You know who you are.
questions.question: In Breaking Bad, what is the street name of Walter White's product?
questions.hostCommentary: Blue Sky! 99.1% pure, apparently. We're not endorsing the chemistry, just the television.

questions.hostIntro: This is a trick question disguised as a normal question. You've been warned.
questions.question: How many Infinity Stones are there in the Marvel Cinematic Universe?
questions.hostCommentary: SIX! Space, Mind, Reality, Power, Time, and Soul. And yes, we know you just counted on your fingers.

questions.hostIntro: Put on your thinking ears. This one separates the casual listeners from the obsessives.
questions.question: Which artist has won the most Grammy Awards ever?
questions.hostCommentary: BEYONCÉ with 32 Grammys! Georg Solti held the record for decades with 31. The queen has spoken.

questions.hostIntro: This is the most important question in the history of human civilization.
questions.question: What year did YouTube launch?
questions.hostCommentary: 2005! And within six months the whole thing was cat videos. As it was written, so it shall be.

questions.hostIntro: The writers of this show want you to feel something. The question wants you to know something.
questions.question: In The Office (US), what is the name of the paper company Dunder Mifflin's parent company?
questions.hostCommentary: SABRE! Named after a fighter jet, run by a Florida company. Gabe would like to remind you of this.

questions.hostIntro: Words incoming. Connect them to the clue and buzz in FAST. Being wrong costs you. Being slow costs everyone patience.
questions.question: OSCAR BEST PICTURE WINNERS
questions.hostCommentary: Titanic, Parasite, Argo, Braveheart, Spotlight, and Gladiator all won Best Picture. Avatar and Inception — nominated but robbed, depending on who you ask.

questions.hostIntro: Movie or TV Show? This should be easy. It is not easy.
questions.question: Big Screen or Small Screen?
questions.hostCommentary: The Wire, Severance, Succession, and Fargo are TV shows. Inception, Dune, The Dark Knight, and Oppenheimer are movies. Yes, Fargo is both. We went with the show.

questions.hostIntro: True or false, pop culture edition. Up for true, down for false. Sixty seconds. Embarrass yourself, or don't.
questions.question: True or False? Pop Culture Edition!
answers: SpongeBob SquarePants lives in a pineapple under the sea
Disney's Frozen is based on Cinderella
Harry Potter was sorted into Ravenclaw
Game of Thrones won the Emmy for Outstanding Drama Series in its final season
Stan Lee had a cameo in every MCU film until his death
The Lion King is set in Kenya
Batman's parents were named Thomas and Martha
The Mandalorian's character name is revealed to be Jake
questions.hostCommentary: How'd you do? Frozen is based on The Snow Queen. Harry was in Gryffindor. GOT's final season was notoriously snubbed. The Lion King is set in the Pride Lands — fictional Africa. Mando's name is Din Djarin.

questions.hostIntro: The image is revealing itself. Jump in early for big points, or wait until you're sure. Totally your choice. No wrong answers. Except the actual wrong answer.
questions.question: Which movie does this iconic poster belong to?
answers: Close Encounters of the Third Kind
questions.hostCommentary: E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial (1982)! That bicycle silhouette in front of the moon is one of the most iconic images in cinema history.

questions.hostIntro: One of these facts is true. The other three are there to make you doubt yourself.
questions.question: Michael Jackson's 'Thriller' album was released in what year?
questions.hostCommentary: 1982! It remains the best-selling album of all time. Over 70 million copies. The zombie dance is still undefeated.
